summary: Even without detailed audio notes, this recording delivers a high‑energy set highlighted by a bass‑heavy Truckin', crisp Eyes jam, and an epic Wharf Rat coda.
review: Both reviewers rave about the musical flow of this show – a thunderous, bass‑driven Truckin' that launches into a twisting transition jam, a tight Eyes performance (“nailed it”), and a satisfying Wharf Rat coda after the Other One saga. No comments were made about sound quality, so the recording’s technical merits remain unclear.

ai_rating.rationale: Two enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the performance heavily; lack of audio commentary prevents a higher confidence level, but the show appears exceptional.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers did not discuss clarity, mix, or source type, focusing solely on musical content.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight fluid transitions – a bass‑heavy jam out of Truckin' leading into an intricate sequence through The Other One, Big River (subdued), Eyes and finally a Wharf Rat coda – indicating strong pacing and improvisational flow.

band_member_comments.Phil: Bass-heavy jam on Truckin' noted, suggesting Phil Lesh’s driving low end was a highlight.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s rhythm work described as “eyeballing Me & My Uncle out of Eyes jam,” indicating active involvement in the transition.
